response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with notable geospatial experience (GISCollective, Green Map) and low-latency systems work at Sociomantic, but the required core skill — modern C++ or Rust — is essentially absent. Their C/C++ exposure dates to 2007-2008 at National Instruments, and recent work centers on TypeScript/Node.js and D. Domain alignment in GIS is real, yet there is no evidence of navigation-grade pipelines or routing tile experience. Worth a short screen only if the team is open to ramping a strong generalist on C++/Rust.
strengths: Long-running hands-on GIS/geospatial platform ownership (GISCollective, Green Map)
Low-latency, high-throughput backend experience at Sociomantic (Dlang, async I/O, fibers)
Polyglot senior engineer with strong testing/TDD discipline and OS/distributed familiarity
concerns: No demonstrated modern C++ or Rust experience in the last ~15 years
No navigation/routing or map-tile pipeline background
Recent trajectory is web/AI tooling, not systems-level data pipelines
